issues.description: OpenAI stateful chaining was not failure-atomic: OpenAIBinding.request advanced self._sent_upto before the HTTP call/raise_for_status/json() succeeded, so a failed request on the same binding instance could skip unsent transcript items while previous_response_id was stale or absent (a multi-turn desync). Not a demonstrated run-path failure (pull_route aborts on exception and builds a fresh binding per run), but a real robustness hole in the stateful binding.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of OpenAIBinding.request stateful cursor handling.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A request whose HTTP call raises must leave _sent_upto and _response_id unchanged so a retry resends rather than skips.
issues.resolution_notes: Staged the cursor as new_upto and committed _response_id/_sent_upto only after a successful response (and, after R2, only after _from_response parsing also succeeds). Tests: test_offset_not_advanced_on_request_failure (R1 fix) and test_offset_not_advanced_on_parse_failure (R2 follow-on). Fully resolved by round 3.

issues.description: The risky OpenAI stateful-offset path (a text-only assistant turn makes the driver append a user NUDGE, not a tool result; the cursor must advance across that extra user turn) was not directly pinned by an OpenAI-binding test. test_request_uses_responses_api_and_chains_previous_id covered only the tool-result follow-up, and test_text_only_turn_nudges_then_verdict exercised FakeBinding, not the real serializer.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of OpenAI test coverage vs the stateful serializer.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Three sequential request() calls on one OpenAIBinding instance: user -> text-only assistant + user nudge -> tool call + result; assert each turn sends only the new items and chains previous_response_id.
issues.resolution_notes: Added test_stateful_offset_advances_across_text_only_nudge pinning the exact nudge path on one instance.

issues.description: Gemini's id-less positional matching (functionCall in a model turn <-> functionResponse in a user turn, matched by order because Gemini provides no wire id) was only tested for a SINGLE functionCall. The adversarial case of multiple same-name calls in one turn (e.g. two read_file calls) was unverified, and the gemini-3* thinkingLevel branch was untested (only the non-gemini-3 thinkingBudget path was exercised).
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider review of Gemini test coverage vs the positional-matching and thinking-config branches.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A Gemini response with two same-name functionCall parts must yield distinct synthesized ids and functionResponse parts in results order; a gemini-3 request must use thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel not thinkingBudget.
